commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Ships; not perhaps from Tyre, but from the Red Sea. (Calmet) Others think that he sent them by a canal, which opened a communication between the Mediterranean and Suez, (Huet) the distance of about ninety miles. ) But this canal seems to have been made after the age of Solomon. Hence others imagine that the ships were taken in pieces, or conveyed by land, as has been sometimes done. Mahomet II transported ships across the isthmus of Corinth. Alexander the Great conveyed on chariots the ships which had been used to cross the Indus, as far as the Hydaspes. (Arrian vii. ) (Calmet) Skilful mariners. They were the most expert, and the inventers of navigation. Prima ratem ventis tradere docta Tyrus. ) See Wisdom xiv. Ophir, the kingdom of Pegu, in the East Indies, (Tirinus) or some other distant land. (Haydock) Fifty. Thirty are omitted, 3 Kings ix. , as they were expended in the voyage. (Calmet) Yet the Hebrew letters for twenty and fifty are extremely similar.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
